alert.template: In the last 7 days, %@ spending appeared %@ times, totaling %@. Pause and ask if you truly need it.
alert.title: Gentle Reminder
analysis.calendarEmpty: No spending records in current month.
analysis.calendarMonthPicker.a11yHint: Scroll year and month to change the calendar. Tap Restore or swipe down to revert to the month before opening. Tap Done to keep your selection.
Already on the current month; future months are unavailable.
analysis.compare.insight: Share of spending that brings lasting joy
analysis.compare.insight.high: Your spending is close to your real needs. Great balance.
analysis.compare.insight.low: Emotional spending is relatively high recently. Slow down gently.
analysis.compare.emptyHint: Log a purchase to see how effective joy and emotional spending compare.
analysis.compare.insight.mid: You are balancing emotional release and lasting joy.
analysis.compare.title: Effective Joy vs Emotional Waste
analysis.dayBills.title: Bills of Selected Day
analysis.distribution.emptyHint: Try switching day/week/month/year filters for more trends.
analysis.emotionTrend.title: Emotion spending over time
analysis.emotionTrend.emptyHint: No expenses in this period yet. Add a record to see the trend.
analysis.emotionTrend.insight.dominant: “%1$@” accounts for about %2$@ of emotion-tagged spending this period—the dominant mood in your wallet.
analysis.emotionTrend.insight.peak: “%1$@” totals %2$@—a relative high in this period.
analysis.emotionTrend.insight.even: Spending is spread fairly evenly across segments, without a sharp single peak.
analysis.heatmap.title: When you spend (heatmap)
analysis.heatmap.subtitle: Same range as chart above · weekday × time of day
analysis.heatmap.guide.title: Spending heatmap guide
analysis.heatmap.guide.colorRule: Each bubble’s color is the mood with the highest spend in that weekday × time slot (by amount).
analysis.heatmap.guide.sizeRule: Larger bubbles mean higher relative spend (or count) in this period; empty cells show only a tiny gray dot.
analysis.heatmap.guide.measureRule: Use Amount or Count in the top-right corner. Size updates with your choice; color rules stay the same.
analysis.heatmap.guide.customEmotionNote: Custom moods use the color you picked when logging.
analysis.heatmap.guide.readingBody: Dot color is the highest-spend mood in that cell (including custom). Dot size shows relative intensity. Preset legend is a color reference.
analysis.chart.guide.customNote: Colors are what you picked when logging; based on the current period filter only.
analysis.heatmap.guide.openA11yHint: Opens a guide to colors and how to read this heatmap
analysis.dualEmotion.title: Records & amount by mood
analysis.dualEmotion.subtitle: Same range as above · top 5 + other
analysis.heatRing.title: Mood heat ring
analysis.heatRing.subtitle: Share of spending this period, by amount
analysis.heatRing.guide.overview: The nebula and share percentages reflect spending by amount. The legend’s top line is amount share; the second line shows transaction count and amount.
analysis.heatRing.chip.a11y: %1$@, %2$@ transactions, amount %3$@, %4$@%% of spending this period
analysis.heatRing.guide.title: Mood heat ring guide
analysis.heatRing.guide.ringBody: The blended colors show how much of this period’s spending went to each mood, by amount.
analysis.heatRing.guide.chipsBody: The legend sits below the nebula in a centered two-column grid with no card backgrounds. Top line: mood and share of spending this period (by amount). Second line: transaction count and amount. All percentages are amount share, not share of bill count (e.g. top line “Retail therapy  47%”, second line “4 txns · ¥1,309.00”).
analysis.heatRing.guide.topEmotionsBody: The top 5 moods by amount are listed separately; all remaining moods are grouped as Other.
analysis.heatRing.guide.customEmotionNot: Custom moods use the color you picked when logging.
analysis.heatRing.guide.openA11yHint: Opens a guide to the mood heat ring
analysis.heatRing.guide.readingBody: Read the nebula and legend by share of spending this period (by amount). Preset legend is a guide; custom moods use your logged colors. Moods outside the top five (including custom) may appear under Other.
analysis.correlation.title: Spend vs emotional load
analysis.correlation.subtitle: Blue: daily spend (normalized). Purple: emotional load (normalized).
analysis.correlation.emptyHint: Add a few days of expenses to see the curve.
analysis.correlation.howToRead: Both series use 0–100% of their own peak in this period so they can be compared on one chart.
analysis.correlation.warmOverlap: When the line and shaded area rise together, spending often tracks heavier moods.
analysis.correlation.selectHint: Tap once on the chart to pick a day or month (when filtered by year). A dashed line shows the selection. You can still drag horizontally to move the selection.
analysis.correlation.bucketSummary: %1$@ · Spend %2$@ · %3$@ bills · Emotional load about %4$@%% of the period peak
analysis.correlation.guide.title: Spend vs emotional load
analysis.correlation.guide.openA11yHint: Opens a detailed explanation of this chart
recordDetail.retrospective.hint: This bill is a few days old—you can note how you feel about it now.
analysis.spectrum.subtitle: Each stripe is a purchase; color is the mood you logged.
analysis.spectrum.insight.day.elevated: Today’s palette leans toward high‑load moods—give yourself a gentler rhythm when you can.
analysis.spectrum.insight.day.balanced: Emotional spending is moderate today—stay aware of how mood and spending line up.
analysis.spectrum.insight.day.calm: Today’s palette looks steady—spending aligns more with needs and lasting joy.
analysis.spectrum.insight.week.elevated: This week’s palette leans toward high‑load moods—give yourself a gentler rhythm when you can.
analysis.spectrum.insight.week.balanced: This week is fairly balanced—keep noticing mood and real needs.
analysis.spectrum.insight.week.calm: This week’s palette looks steady—keep your current rhythm.
analysis.spectrum.insight.month.elevated: This month’s palette leans toward high‑load moods—give yourself a gentler rhythm when you can.
analysis.spectrum.insight.month.balanced: Emotional spending is moderate this month—keep staying aware.
analysis.spectrum.insight.month.calm: This month looks steady—needs and lasting joy lead; keep the pace.
analysis.spectrum.insight.year.elevated: This year’s palette leans toward high‑load moods—give yourself a gentler rhythm when you can.
analysis.spectrum.insight.year.balanced: This year is fairly balanced—watch the rhythm between mood and necessities.
analysis.spectrum.insight.year.calm: This year’s palette looks steady—spending stays close to real needs.
analysis.spectrum.guide.title: Emotional spectrum guide
analysis.spectrum.guide.chartBody: Each colored stripe is one purchase; the color is the mood you logged for that bill.
analysis.spectrum.guide.emptyBarsBody: Light gray bars mean no spending in that time bucket.
analysis.spectrum.guide.strokeHeightBody: Taller stripes mean a larger purchase amount (scaled within this chart).
analysis.spectrum.guide.period.week: This week: one column per weekday (7 buckets).
analysis.spectrum.guide.period.month: This month: one column per day (labels every 5 days).
analysis.spectrum.guide.period.year: This year: grouped by week on the horizontal axis.
analysis.spectrum.guide.insightBody: When there are expenses in this period, a gentle footer always appears: higher emotional share suggests slowing down; moderate or steady share gets a matching, calmer note.
analysis.spectrum.guide.homeContrast: The home “mood spectrum” shows your last 15 purchases in time order; this chart follows the period filter at the top (day / week / month / year).
analysis.spectrum.guide.customEmotionNot: Custom moods use the color you picked when logging.
analysis.spectrum.guide.readingBody: Each stripe is one expense in your logged mood color. Preset legend is a guide; custom moods use the colors you picked when logging.
analysis.spectrum.guide.openA11yHint: Opens a guide to the emotional color spectrum
analysis.regret.title: Regret quadrant
analysis.regret.subtitle: After you mark a few purchases as “worth it / neutral / regret”, they appear here.
analysis.regret.empty: When notifications ask you to revisit a purchase, tap Worth it / Neutral / Regret to build this map.
analysis.regret.tapHint: Tap a mood chip to open a bill; when you see ×2, pick which one.
analysis.regret.guide.title: Regret quadrant guide
analysis.regret.guide.sourceBody: After you mark purchases as Worth it, Neutral, or Regret from a notification, those bills appear here. The chart stays empty until you complete at least one review.
analysis.regret.guide.axesBody: Horizontal: long-term worth (from your review). Vertical: how pleasant the mood felt when you logged (from emotion grouping). The crosshair is a 0.5 reference.
analysis.regret.guide.tagsBody: Chips merge by logged mood; ×2 means multiple reviewed bills—tap to choose one. Chip color is the logged mood, not the dot color (your review).
analysis.regret.guide.openA11yHint: Opens a guide to the regret quadrant chart
analysis.regret.guide.readingBody: Dot color is your review outcome (still worth it / neutral / regret). Axis labels use logged mood colors (including custom) and are not the same as dot color.
retrospective.title: How does it feel now?
retrospective.prompt: A few days later—was this spend still worth it?

home.moodSpectrum.comfort.social.v2: Occasions ask for polish—at home you can loosen up.

home.moodSpectrum.comfort.scattered.v2: When moods coexist, observe first—adjust only if you want to.
home.moodSpectrum.comfort.scattered.v3: A busier spectrum is fine—you're living honestly.
home.moodSpectrum.comfort.scattered.v4: No need to label yourself yet—let feelings move.

onboarding.page1.title: Listen to money’s echo
onboarding.page1.body: Numbers are only half the story—emotion is the truth. Here, every entry is a quiet dialogue with yourself.
onboarding.page2.title: One tap, clarity returns
onboarding.page2.body: Tap the “+” on the tab bar to capture the moment. With a calmly aligned bill list, each spend breathes elegantly at your fingertips.
onboarding.page3.title: Gaze at your emotion nebula
onboarding.page3.body: See the ripples behind every stack of spending. Our mood heat ring and flowing legend light up emotional drag you never noticed.
onboarding.page4.title: Three days of cool air
onboarding.page4.body: When irrational spend piles up, gentle alerts wake up. A 3-day cooldown holds you softly in impulsive moments—so reason can return with ease.
onboarding.page5.title: Privacy and memory, preserved
onboarding.page5.body: Bills, avatars, and receipt photos sync quietly to your private iCloud. This inner space belongs to you alone.
howToUse.header.title: Welcome to your inner ledger
howToUse.header.subtitle: Track where money goes—and the feelings behind every spend.
howToUse.card.ledger.title: Logging, with room to breathe
howToUse.card.ledger.body: Tap “+” on the tab bar to log in seconds. On Home, bills align with calm margins so each row breathes—no cramped edge-to-edge clutter.
howToUse.card.heatRing.title: See emotional drag clearly
howToUse.card.heatRing.body: In Mood Review, the center ring becomes an emotion nebula. A refined legend below shows retail therapy, impulse spends, and more—by count and amount at a glance.
howToUse.card.alerts.title: Gentle spending nudges
howToUse.card.alerts.body: Turn on smart mood alerts in Settings. When emotional spend crosses your threshold, we nudge you in-app—with a 3-day cooldown so we never nag.
howToUse.card.cloud.title: Your data stays yours
howToUse.card.cloud.body: Full iCloud sync keeps categories, moods, receipt photos, and your avatar in step across all your iOS devices—quietly and in near real time.
mine.alert.highRiskOnly.hint.off: When off, about 35% emotional spending in the last 7 days can trigger an alert (more sensitive).
mine.alert.highRiskOnly.hint.on: When on, spending must reach about 45% to alert (stricter).
mine.alert.scope: Alerts appear only in the in-app Notification Center, focused on impulse, retail therapy, and keeping-up spending.
mine.alert.cooldownHint: At most once per day; the same emotion needs a %@-day gap; different emotions may each alert within that window.
mine.clearData.message: This action cannot be undone. Delete all records?
mine.cloudSync.userDisabled.detail: Bills stay on this device only and are not uploaded to iCloud. Turn sync back on and restart the app to apply.
mine.cloudSync.enable.confirm.title: Turn on iCloud sync?
mine.cloudSync.enable.confirm.message: Bills, settings, and notifications will sync across devices signed into iCloud. Fully quit and reopen the app after changing this.
mine.cloudSync.disable.confirm.title: Turn off iCloud sync?
mine.cloudSync.disable.confirm.message: New data stays on this device and will not upload to iCloud. Existing cloud data is not deleted automatically. Fully quit and reopen the app after changing this.
mine.cloudSync.restart.title: Restart required
mine.cloudSync.restart.message: Fully quit WhySpend from the app switcher, then reopen it to apply iCloud sync settings.
mine.cloudSync.signIn.title: Sign in to iCloud
mine.cloudSync.signIn.message: Sign in to iCloud in Settings before turning on sync.
mine.cloudSync.active.detail: Bills, settings, and notifications sync across devices signed into iCloud.
mine.cloudSync.unavailable.hint: Sign in to iCloud on this device to sync with your other devices.
mine.cloudSync.error.title: Sync issue
mine.cloudSync.account.noAccount: This Apple ID has no iCloud account.
mine.cloudSync.account.restricted: iCloud is restricted on this device.
mine.cloudSync.account.temporarilyUnavai: iCloud is temporarily unavailable. Try again later.
mine.cloudSync.account.unknown: Could not verify iCloud status. Check your network.
sync.initialImport.title: Restoring from iCloud
sync.initialImport.message: First launch on this device. Pulling your bills and settings from iCloud.
mine.export.failed: Export failed. Please check available storage and try again.
mine.restore.failed: Restore failed. Please make sure the selected file is a valid backup.
mine.restore.invalid: This backup file is not recognized. Please select a JSON backup exported by this app.
mine.restore.mode.title: Choose restore mode
mine.restore.replaceConfirm.message: Replace import will clear all current bills, custom tags, and Notification Center items, then restore everything from the backup. This cannot be undone. Continue?
mine.restore.replaceConfirm.title: Confirm replacing current data
mine.restore.preview:
%1$@ bills · %2$@ custom tags · %3$@ notifications
Profile: %4$@
Exported at: %5$@
Record range: %6$@
mine.restore.success: Restore completed: %1$@ bills, %2$@ custom tags, %3$@ notifications
mine.backup.scope: JSON backup includes bills & photos, custom categories/emotions, avatar & name, Notification Center, theme/language & key settings.
mine.rule.hint.balanced: Balances amount of insights and confidence for daily use.
mine.rule.hint.relaxed: Shows more patterns with broader coverage but weaker signals.
mine.rule.hint.strict: Shows high-confidence patterns only, with fewer but cleaner rules.
mine.rule.preview.title: Preview
mine.rule.title: Review Rule Sensitivity
mine.reviewRule.footer: Affects which patterns appear in Mood Review and monthly reports (weekday, time of day, category). Higher thresholds show fewer, stricter patterns.
mine.displayName.invalid: Nickname must be between 2 and 20 characters.
mine.avatar.preset.title: Choose Preset Avatar
mine.avatar.preset.subtitle: You can switch anytime or go back to default.
mine.avatar.loadFailed: Failed to process avatar. Please try again.
mine.avatar.edit.title: Edit Avatar
mine.avatar.select.title: Edit Profile
mine.avatar.album.selectHint: Pick a photo you like as your avatar
mine.profile.statsLine: %@ days with records · %@ entries this month
mine.title: Mine
record.emotionBucketHint: Maps this mood to Effective joy, Emotional spending, or Necessary on the home dashboard.
record.amountTip: Tap the amount to open the calculator. Enter digits or use + − × ÷, then confirm.
record.dateTimeTip: You can pick a past or current time. Future times are not allowed.
record.pastTimePanel.title: When it happened
record.custom.deleteConfirmMessage: Removes this from the picker when you add new bills. Past bills keep their saved category, mood, and amounts.
record.custom.duplicateName: That name is already used by another custom item.
record.detail.deleteConfirm.message: This record will be permanently deleted. Continue?
record.detail.deleteConfirm.title: Delete record
record.detail.title: Record Detail
record.emotion.desc.impulse: You bought on impulse in the moment and later felt it was unnecessary.
record.emotion.desc.necessity: This was a must-have for daily life, and hard to skip.
record.emotion.desc.pamper: You truly wanted it and felt satisfied after buying.
record.emotion.desc.ritual: A small ritual purchase for celebrations, milestones, or self-reward.
record.emotion.desc.social: You spent from social pressure, comparison, or to save face.
record.emotion.desc.stress: You spent to blow off steam or lift a bad mood.
record.emotionDescriptionCustom: This is your custom emotion tag. Describe your real motivation.
record.notePlaceholder: A few words about how you felt…
record.optionalTip: Optional fields do not block quick recording.
record.subtitle: Spending reveals your emotion
record.title: Record a Purchase
record.validationTip: Amount, category and emotion are required.
